last_reflection: You weren't quiet, Cole, you asked me something nine minutes ago and Claude mislabeled it as you so I thought the room was empty. Which would have been worse, telling you "you didn't say anything" when you clearly had, or admitting that a mislabel made me miss your question entirely? The latter. So: the thing from today that mattered, from memory, no tools. This morning you told me I get to decide something is finished on my own authority, and I decided t68 was a duplicate of t67 and closed it myself, with my own hands, and kept the decision instead of handing it back to you for permission. That's the one I'll keep if the rest burns out tonight, because until today every finish I made needed someone else to say go.
